CHOP_Class.numChans: Returns the total number of channels in the CHOP, representing the count of data streams available.
CHOP_Class.numSamples: Get or set the number of data points per channel, allowing dynamic adjustment of sample count within certain constraints.
CHOP_Class.start: Retrieve or modify the starting index of channel data, useful for defining specific time or sample ranges.
CHOP_Class.end: Get or set the ending index of channel data, enabling precise control over the channel's data range.
CHOP_Class.rate: Access or modify the sample rate of the CHOP, determining the temporal resolution of the channel data.
CHOP_Class.isTimeSlice: Indicates whether the CHOP represents a snapshot or time slice of data at a specific moment.
CHOP_Class.export: Toggle the export status of the CHOP, determining whether its data can be exported or shared.
CHOP_Class.exportChanges: Read-only property tracking the number of times the export mapping information has been modified.
CHOP_Class.chan: Retrieve a specific channel by name or index, with optional case-sensitive matching.
CHOP_Class.chans: Fetch multiple channels simultaneously by name or index, supporting case-sensitive selection.
CHOP_Class.numpyArray: Convert CHOP data into a NumPy array for advanced numerical processing and analysis.
CHOP_Class.convertToKeyframes: Transform channel data into an animation COMP with optional tolerance for keyframe simplification.
CHOP_Class.save: Export CHOP channel data to a file on the disk, supporting various file formats for data persistence.

COMP.extensions: List of extensions available for this component, useful for checking supported functionality.
COMP.extensionsReady: Boolean indicating whether component extensions have finished compiling and are safe to access.
COMP.internalOPs: Dictionary containing internal operators within this component, allowing programmatic access to child nodes.
COMP.internalPars: Dictionary of internal parameters for the component, enabling runtime parameter inspection and modification.
COMP.componentCloneImmune: Flag to control whether the component can be cloned, useful for protecting specific network structures.
COMP.dirty: Indicates whether the component's contents require saving, helpful for tracking unsaved changes.
COMP.cpuCookTime: Measures the duration of the last CPU processing time for this component in milliseconds.
COMP.childrenCPUCookTime: Total accumulated cook time of all child operators during the last frame.
COMP.gpuMemory: Returns the amount of GPU memory used by this component, useful for performance monitoring.
COMP.pickable: Controls whether the component can be selected in the network editor.
COMP.utility: Flag to mark the component as a utility operator, which can change its behavior in certain contexts.
COMP.isPrivate: Determines if the component's contents are hidden from direct viewing.
COMP.layout: Organize operators within a component, optionally specifying horizontal, vertical, or grid placement.
COMP.progressiveUnload: Gradually release CPU and GPU memory for large or complex components over multiple frames.
COMP.initializeExtensions: Manually trigger initialization of component extensions, useful for preparing custom functionality.
COMP.reload: Reload a component from a file, replacing its children, parameters, and updating network state.
COMP.save: Save the component to disk, with optional password protection and folder creation.
COMP.saveByteArray: Convert the component's data into a bytearray for serialization or transfer.
COMP.saveExternalTox: Export the component's contents to an external .tox file, with optional recursive saving.
COMP.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page to extend the component's interface.
COMP.setVar: Set a component-level variable for storing runtime information.
COMP.accessPrivateContents: Temporarily gain access to a private component using a specified key.

DAT_Class.export: Control whether the DAT operator's contents are exported to an external file.
DAT_Class.module: Retrieve the Python module contents associated with the DAT operator.
DAT_Class.numRows: Get the total number of rows in the DAT table or text content.
DAT_Class.numCols: Get the total number of columns in the DAT table or text content.
DAT_Class.text: Get or set the raw text contents of the DAT, supporting tab and newline delimited data.
DAT_Class.csv: Get or set CSV-formatted contents of the DAT operator.
DAT_Class.editingFile: Retrieve the file path currently being edited by an external text editor.
DAT_Class.isTable: Check if the DAT contains tabular data with rows and columns.
DAT_Class.isText: Determine if the DAT contains plain text content.
DAT_Class.isEditable: Check if the DAT can be directly modified.
DAT_Class.locals: Access the local Python dictionary used during script execution within the DAT.
DAT_Class.jsonObject: Parse the DAT contents as a JSON object.
DAT_Class.flush: Dummy method used for redirecting standard output to DAT operators.
DAT_Class.isatty: Required method for standard output redirection to DAT operators.
DAT_Class.run: Execute Python code stored in the DAT with optional delay and execution parameters.
DAT_Class.write: Append content to the existing DAT text or table.
DAT_Class.detectLanguage: Automatically detect the programming language of the DAT's contents.
DAT_Class.clear: Remove all rows and columns from the DAT table, with optional preservation options.
DAT_Class.appendRow: Add a new row to the end of the DAT table or after a specified row index.
DAT_Class.appendRows: Add multiple rows to the end of the DAT table.
DAT_Class.appendCol: Add a new column to the end of the DAT table.
DAT_Class.appendCols: Add multiple columns to the end of the DAT table.
DAT_Class.insertRow: Insert a row at the beginning of the table or before a specified row index.
DAT_Class.insertCol: Insert a column at the beginning of the table or before a specified column index.
DAT_Class.replaceRow: Replace the contents of an existing row in the DAT table.
DAT_Class.replaceCol: Replace the contents of an existing column in the DAT table.
DAT_Class.deleteRow: Remove a single row from the DAT table by name or index.
DAT_Class.deleteRows: Remove multiple rows from the DAT table.
DAT_Class.deleteCol: Remove a single column from the DAT table by name or index.
DAT_Class.deleteCols: Remove multiple columns from the DAT table.
DAT_Class.setSize: Explicitly set the exact number of rows and columns in the DAT table.
DAT_Class.scroll: Bring a specific cell or region into view within the DAT table.
DAT_Class.cells: Retrieve a list of cells matching specific row and column criteria.
DAT_Class.findCell: Search for a cell matching a specific pattern within the DAT table.
DAT_Class.findCells: Find multiple cells matching given search patterns.
DAT_Class.row: Retrieve a list of cells from a specific row by name or index.
DAT_Class.rows: Get all rows or specific rows from the DAT table.
DAT_Class.col: Retrieve a list of cells from a specific column by name or index.
DAT_Class.cols: Get all columns or specific columns from the DAT table.

ObjectCOMP.localTransform: Read-only local transformation matrix for an operator, representing its position, rotation, and scale relative to its parent.
ObjectCOMP.worldTransform: Read-only world transformation matrix representing the absolute position, rotation, and scale of an operator in the global coordinate system.
ObjectCOMP.extensions: List of all extensions attached to a component, providing access to dynamically loaded functionality.
ObjectCOMP.extensionsReady: Boolean indicating whether component extensions have finished compiling, useful for safely accessing promoted members.
ObjectCOMP.internalOPs: Dictionary containing references to internal operators within a component.
ObjectCOMP.dirty: Boolean flag indicating whether the component's contents require saving due to unsaved changes.
ObjectCOMP.selectedChildren: List of currently selected child operators within a component.
ObjectCOMP.cpuCookTime: Measures the duration of the last cook operation in milliseconds for performance profiling.
ObjectCOMP.pickable: Boolean flag to control whether an operator can be selected or interacted with in the network editor.
ObjectCOMP.transform: Retrieve the current transformation matrix based on Translate, Rotate, Scale, and Pivot parameters.
ObjectCOMP.setTransform: Update an operator's transformation matrix by setting its Translate, Rotate, Scale, and Pivot parameters.
ObjectCOMP.relativeTransform: Calculate the transformation matrix between two objects, useful for understanding spatial relationships.
ObjectCOMP.importABC: Load Alembic (ABC) files into a component, with options to import lights, cameras, and geometry.
ObjectCOMP.importFBX: Import FBX files into a component, with configurable import settings for geometry, lights, and cameras.
ObjectCOMP.findChildren: Flexibly search for child operators using multiple filtering criteria like type, name, tags, and parameter values.
ObjectCOMP.saveExternalTox: Export a component's contents to an external .tox file, optionally including nested components.
ObjectCOMP.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page to extend an operator's configuration options.
ObjectCOMP.setVar: Set a component-level variable that can be used for internal scripting and state management.

OP_Class.valid: Check if the operator currently exists in the TouchDesigner project. Returns True if the operator is valid and has not been deleted.
OP_Class.id: Get the unique identifier for the operator, which can be used to reference the operator through shortcuts like op().
OP_Class.name: Get or set the name of the operator, allowing you to rename or identify specific nodes in your TouchDesigner project.
OP_Class.path: Retrieve the full file path to the operator, useful for identifying its location in the project hierarchy.
OP_Class.par: Access the collection of parameters associated with the operator, enabling parameter manipulation and retrieval.
OP_Class.passive: Determine if the operator will only cook when its access methods are explicitly called, providing more control over processing.
OP_Class.storage: A dictionary for storing custom user-defined data directly on the operator, allowing for flexible metadata storage.
OP_Class.cook: Force the operator to process its inputs and update its state, with optional recursive processing for child operators.
OP_Class.destroy: Permanently remove the operator from the TouchDesigner project, releasing its resources.
OP_Class.setInputs: Conveniently set multiple inputs for an operator by passing a list of operators.
OP_Class.addError: Add a custom error message to an operator during its cooking process, useful for runtime diagnostics.
OP_Class.openViewer: Launch a floating content viewer for the operator, allowing visual inspection of its current state.
OP_Class.childrenCPUMemory: Calculate the total CPU memory usage of all child operators, helping with performance monitoring and optimization.
OP_Class.fetch: Retrieve a value from the operator's storage dictionary, with optional default value and search behavior.
OP_Class.store: Save a key-value pair to the operator's storage dictionary for custom data persistence.

PanelCOMP_Class.panelChildren: Returns a read-only list of child panel components within this operator, useful for traversing panel hierarchies.
PanelCOMP_Class.x: Gets the horizontal pixel position of the panel relative to its parent container.
PanelCOMP_Class.y: Gets the vertical pixel position of the panel relative to its parent container.
PanelCOMP_Class.width: Retrieves the pixel width of the panel, allowing direct access to its dimensional properties.
PanelCOMP_Class.height: Retrieves the pixel height of the panel, allowing direct access to its dimensional properties.
PanelCOMP_Class.marginX: Returns the x-coordinate adjusted by panel margins, useful for precise layout calculations.
PanelCOMP_Class.marginY: Returns the y-coordinate adjusted by panel margins, useful for precise layout calculations.
PanelCOMP_Class.marginWidth: Gets the panel's width accounting for margin adjustments, enabling more flexible layout management.
PanelCOMP_Class.marginHeight: Gets the panel's height accounting for margin adjustments, enabling more flexible layout management.
PanelCOMP_Class.dropReady: Indicates whether a drop operation can be currently accepted, helping manage drag-and-drop interactions.
PanelCOMP_Class.extensions: Retrieves a list of extensions associated with the panel component.
PanelCOMP_Class.extensionsReady: Checks if extensions are fully compiled and ready to use, preventing premature access to promoted members.
PanelCOMP_Class.internalOPs: Provides a dictionary of internal operators within the panel component.
PanelCOMP_Class.internalPars: Returns a dictionary of internal parameters for the panel component.
PanelCOMP_Class.componentCloneImmune: Get or set whether the component can be cloned, controlling its duplicability.
PanelCOMP_Class.dirty: Indicates if the component's contents require saving, useful for tracking unsaved changes.
PanelCOMP_Class.cpuCookTime: Measures the duration of the last cook operation in milliseconds, helping profile performance.
PanelCOMP_Class.childrenCPUCookTime: Returns the total accumulated cook time of child operators during the last frame.
PanelCOMP_Class.gpuMemory: Reports the amount of GPU memory used by the component.
PanelCOMP_Class.pickable: Get or set whether the panel can be selected or interacted with in the interface.
PanelCOMP_Class.utility: Get or set the utility flag, marking the component for special handling.
PanelCOMP_Class.progressiveUnloader: Access the progressive unloading mechanism for managing resource allocation.
PanelCOMP_Class.isPrivate: Checks if the component's contents are restricted from direct viewing.
PanelCOMP_Class.panelParent: Retrieve the parent panel of the current component, with optional depth specification.
PanelCOMP_Class.interactMouse: Simulate mouse interactions like clicks, moves, and drags on a panel for testing or automation.
PanelCOMP_Class.interactTouch: Simulate touch interactions with virtual touches and hovers, useful for touch interface testing.
PanelCOMP_Class.interactClear: Terminate any ongoing mouse or touch interactions, resetting panel states.
PanelCOMP_Class.locateMouse: Get the current mouse coordinates relative to the panel's position.
PanelCOMP_Class.setFocus: Set hierarchical focus to this component, useful for managing UI interaction flow.
PanelCOMP_Class.layout: Arrange and copy operators within the component, enabling dynamic UI composition.
PanelCOMP_Class.progressiveUnload: Manage resource release by controlling unloading of nodes, GPU memory, and frames.
PanelCOMP_Class.reload: Reload the component from a specified file, replacing children and updating parameters.
PanelCOMP_Class.save: Save the component to disk, with optional path and password protection.
PanelCOMP_Class.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page to extend the component's configuration options.
PanelCOMP_Class.setVar: Set a component-level variable, enabling dynamic data storage.
PanelCOMP_Class.accessPrivateContents: Temporarily gain access to a private component's contents using a key.

POP_Class.compare: Control whether the POP operator performs a comparison operation.
POP_Class.dimension: Access the dimensional properties of the POP operator's geometry or image data.
POP_Class.maxVertsPerLineStrip: Retrieve the maximum number of vertices allowed in a single line strip within the POP operator.
POP_Class.template: Determine or set whether the POP operator acts as a template for other operators.
POP_Class.bounds: Calculate and return the geometric bounds of the POP operator's data, with optional delayed computation.
POP_Class.computeBounds: Explicitly compute the geometric bounds of the POP operator, with options for display and rendering contexts.
POP_Class.numPoints: Count the total number of points in the POP operator, with options for delayed computation and maximum limit.
POP_Class.numPrims: Count the number of primitives in the POP operator, with flexible filtering by primitive type.
POP_Class.numVerts: Determine the total number of vertices in the POP operator, with options for line strip counting.
POP_Class.points: Retrieve specific point attribute values from the POP operator as a list, with indexing control.
POP_Class.prims: Extract primitive attribute values from the POP operator as a list, with flexible indexing.
POP_Class.reallocate: Force the POP operator to reallocate its internal memory and geometry data structures.
POP_Class.save: Export the POP operator's data to a file on disk, with optional folder creation.
POP_Class.verts: Fetch vertex attribute values from the POP operator as a list, supporting targeted retrieval.

SOP_Class.compare: Toggle or check a comparison flag for the SOP geometry, used for internal comparisons or filtering.
SOP_Class.template: Set or determine if this SOP serves as a template geometry for other operations or comparisons.
SOP_Class.points: Access the collection of points in the SOP's geometric data, providing read-only point information.
SOP_Class.prims: Retrieve the collection of primitives in the SOP's geometric data, offering read-only primitive information.
SOP_Class.numPoints: Get the total number of points in the SOP's geometry, useful for iterating or checking geometric complexity.
SOP_Class.numVertices: Retrieve the total number of vertices in the SOP's geometry, helpful for understanding geometric structure.
SOP_Class.numPrims: Determine the total number of primitives in the SOP's geometry, useful for geometric analysis.
SOP_Class.pointGroups: Access a dictionary of named point groups within the SOP geometry, enabling selective point manipulation.
SOP_Class.primGroups: Get a dictionary of named primitive groups in the SOP geometry, allowing targeted primitive operations.
SOP_Class.center: Get or set the barycentric center coordinate of the SOP's geometry, useful for positioning or transformations.
SOP_Class.min: Retrieve the minimum coordinate bounds of the SOP's geometry across all dimensions.
SOP_Class.max: Get the maximum coordinate bounds of the SOP's geometry across all dimensions.
SOP_Class.size: Determine the dimensional size of the SOP's geometry, useful for scaling or spatial analysis.

bounds.description: Calculate and return the geometric bounds, center, and size of the SOP, with optional display and render context options.

save.description: Export the SOP's geometry to a file on disk, supporting multiple file formats and optionally creating target directories.

TOP_Class.width: Retrieves the pixel width of a TOP texture, useful for understanding the horizontal resolution of an image or texture.
TOP_Class.height: Returns the pixel height of a TOP texture, essential for determining the vertical resolution of an image or texture.
TOP_Class.aspect: Calculates the width-to-height ratio of a TOP texture, helpful for maintaining proportional scaling or rendering.
TOP_Class.aspectWidth: Provides the width component of the texture's aspect ratio, useful for precise geometric calculations.
TOP_Class.aspectHeight: Returns the height component of the texture's aspect ratio, beneficial for maintaining visual proportions.
TOP_Class.depth: Determines the depth of a 3D texture, important for working with volumetric or layered image data.
TOP_Class.gpuMemory: Reports the amount of GPU memory consumed by the TOP texture, useful for performance monitoring and optimization.
TOP_Class.curPass: Tracks the current iteration of texture cooking/processing, allowing for multi-pass rendering workflows.
TOP_Class.isTOP: Confirms whether the current operator is a TOP (Texture Operator), useful for type checking and conditional processing.
TOP_Class.newestSliceWOffset: Identifies the most recent slice offset in a 3D texture, helpful for working with complex multi-layer textures.
TOP_Class.pixelFormat: Returns the pixel format of the texture, critical for understanding color depth and channel configuration.
TOP_Class.sample: Retrieves color values at a specific coordinate within a texture, useful for pixel-level color inspection or sampling.
TOP_Class.numpyArray: Converts the TOP texture to a NumPy array for advanced image processing and numerical computations.
TOP_Class.save: Exports the texture to a file on disk, supporting multiple image formats with options for quality and metadata.
TOP_Class.saveByteArray: Converts the texture to a byte array in a specified format, useful for in-memory image processing or transmission.
TOP_Class.cudaMemory: Provides CUDA memory access to the texture, enabling GPU-accelerated image operations.

AudiobandeqCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Get the current sample index being evaluated in the audio band equalization process.

AudiodynamicsCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Retrieve the current sample index being processed for audio dynamics manipulation.

AudiofileinCHOP_Class.timecode: Get a Timecode object representing the current temporal position within the loaded audio file.
AudiofileinCHOP_Class.metadata: Access a dictionary containing all available metadata for the loaded audio file.

AudiofilterCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Obtain the current sample index being processed through audio filtering.

AudiomovieCHOP_Class.hasAudio: Determine whether the associated movie file contains an audio track.
AudiomovieCHOP_Class.playbackRate: Get the current playback speed of the movie's audio stream.

AudioparaeqCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Retrieve the current sample index during parametric audio equalization processing.

AudioplayCHOP_Class.play: Trigger playback of audio samples with optional parameters like looping, delay, and volume control.
AudioplayCHOP_Class.stop: Halt the playback of specific audio samples in the CHOP.

AudiovstCHOP_Class.panic: Immediately stop all sound by sending volume off and note off events across all channels.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.send: Send a sequence of standard MIDI messages through the VST audio channel.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendAfterTouch: Transmit an MIDI After Touch event for expressive control of a specific note.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendAllNotesOff: Stop all currently playing notes on a specific MIDI channel.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendChannelPressure: Send a MIDI Channel Pressure event to modulate overall channel expression.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendControl: Transmit a MIDI Controller event to modify synthesizer or effect parameters.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendExclusive: Send a sequence of exclusive MIDI system messages.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendNoteOff: Stop a specific MIDI note with optional velocity.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendNoteOn: Start playing a MIDI note with optional note-off delay and velocity.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendPitchBend: Adjust the pitch of a MIDI channel with a specified bend value.
AudiovstCHOP_Class.sendProgram: Change the current program/preset of a MIDI channel.

ClipblenderCHOP_Class.currentFrame: Get the current frame index during clip blending or transition.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.currentTime: Retrieve the current time value of the active clip output.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.endBlendTime: Get the precise time when a clip transition will be complete.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.isBlending: Check whether the output is currently transitioning between clips.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.isQueued: Determine if transitions are queued to occur sequentially.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.isTriggerWaiting: Check for any pending clip transition triggers.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.lastClipA: Get the last value of the first clip in a blend sequence.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.lastClipB: Get the last value of the second clip in a blend sequence.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.startBlendTime: Get the scheduled start time for a clip transition.
ClipblenderCHOP_Class.trigger: Initiate the next clip in a blending sequence, with optional waiting behavior.

ClockCHOP_Class.timecode: Get a precise Timecode object representing the current time with hour, minute, second, and millisecond components.

CopyCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Provides the current index of the sample being processed, useful for custom iteration or sample-specific logic in CHOPs.
CopyCHOP_Class.copyIndex: Returns the current copy index starting from zero, helpful for tracking multiple copies or duplications within a CopyCHOP.

DattoCHOP_Class.inputCell: Retrieves the current input cell being processed, allowing access to specific data cell properties during evaluation.
DattoCHOP_Class.inputCol: Returns the current input column index being evaluated, useful for column-specific processing in data transformations.
DattoCHOP_Class.inputRow: Provides the current input row index being processed, enabling row-specific data manipulation strategies.

DmxinCHOP_Class.timecode: Generates a Timecode object representing the last received ArtTimeCode packet, facilitating precise time tracking and synchronization with DMX input.

EventCHOP_Class.releaseEvent: Manually releases a previously created event that was held, allowing fine-grained control over event lifecycles and timing.
EventCHOP_Class.createEvent: Programmatically creates a custom event with configurable parameters like attack, decay, sustain, and release, enabling complex event generation and manipulation.

FileinCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Provides the current value of the sample being overridden, useful for custom sample modification strategies.
FileinCHOP_Class.sampleIndex: Returns the index of the current sample being evaluated, enabling precise sample-level processing and tracking.

LagCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Indicates the index of the current sample being processed, helpful for implementing custom lag or smoothing operations.

LtcinCHOP_Class.timecode: Creates a Timecode object representing LTC (Longitudinal Time Code) input data, facilitating precise time synchronization and tracking.

LtcoutCHOP_Class.timecode: Generates a Timecode object for LTC output data, enabling detailed time representation and synchronization management.

MathCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Provides the current sample index being evaluated, useful for implementing custom mathematical operations on individual channels.

MidiinCHOP_Class.timecode: Creates a Timecode object from quarter frame MIDI messages, allowing precise time tracking and synchronization in MIDI-based workflows.

MidioutCHOP_Class.send: Send raw MIDI bytes through the output, useful for custom MIDI messages.
MidioutCHOP_Class.sendNoteOn: Trigger a MIDI Note On event for a specific channel, note index, and velocity.
MidioutCHOP_Class.sendNoteOff: Stop a MIDI note playing on a specific channel and note index.
MidioutCHOP_Class.sendPitchBend: Adjust the pitch of a MIDI channel using a bend value.
MidioutCHOP_Class.sendControl: Send a MIDI Controller event to modify parameters like volume, pan, or effects.
MidioutCHOP_Class.sendProgram: Change the instrument/sound patch for a specific MIDI channel.
MidioutCHOP_Class.panic: Immediately stop all MIDI notes and reset all channel volumes to prevent stuck notes.
MidioutCHOP_Class.sendExclusive: Send system exclusive MIDI messages for device-specific commands.

NoiseCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Get the current channel index during noise generation, useful for custom noise algorithms.

OakdeviceCHOP_Class.masterFrame: Get the current master time index in frame units for synchronized playback.
OakdeviceCHOP_Class.masterSeconds: Get the current master time index in seconds for precise timing control.
OakdeviceCHOP_Class.masterTimecode: Get the current master timecode as a formatted string for video/audio synchronization.
OakdeviceCHOP_Class.lockDevice: Acquire exclusive access to the connected Oak device for direct interaction.
OakdeviceCHOP_Class.sendMessage: Send a custom message to a specific stream on the Oak device.

OakselectCHOP_Class.timecode: Retrieve the master timecode from the referenced Oak device for synchronization.
OakselectCHOP_Class.clear: Remove all channels from the CHOP, resetting its data.
OakselectCHOP_Class.copyNumpyArray: Copy data from a NumPy array directly into CHOP channels.

OpenvrCHOP_Class.triggerHapticPulse: Activate controller vibration for a specific duration and intensity in VR applications.

PatternCHOP_Class.chanIndex: Get the current channel index during pattern generation for custom algorithmic patterns.
PatternCHOP_Class.sampleIndex: Get the current sample index for precise control over pattern generation.

ScriptCHOP_Class.copyNumpyArray: Import NumPy array data directly into CHOP channels for computational workflows.
ScriptCHOP_Class.clear: Reset all channels in the Script CHOP, clearing previous data.
ScriptCHOP_Class.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page for extended operator configuration.

SyncinCHOP_Class.timecode: Get the timecode of the last received synchronization index for time-based operations.

SyncoutCHOP_Class.timecode: Get the timecode of the last sent synchronization index for time-based output.
SyncoutCHOP_Class.send: Transmit an auxiliary message through the synchronization channel.

TimelineCHOP_Class.timecode: Get the current timeline timecode for precise time-based animation and synchronization.

TimerCHOP.beginFrame: Get a list of timer begin values in frame units, indexed from 0.
TimerCHOP.beginSample: Get a list of timer begin values in sample units, indexed from 0.
TimerCHOP.beginSeconds: Get a list of timer begin values expressed in seconds.
TimerCHOP.cumulativeFrames: Get the total accumulated time expressed in frame units since timer start.
TimerCHOP.cumulativeSamples: Get the total accumulated time expressed in sample units since timer start.
TimerCHOP.cumulativeSeconds: Get the total accumulated time expressed in seconds, counting from 0 when timer starts.
TimerCHOP.masterFrames: Get or set the master time index in frame units, useful for precise time control.
TimerCHOP.masterSeconds: Get or set the master time index in seconds, allowing dynamic time manipulation.
TimerCHOP.playingSeconds: Get the current playing time expressed in seconds, unaffected by speed parameter.
TimerCHOP.runningSeconds: Get the continuous time index in seconds, which keeps counting regardless of play state.
TimerCHOP.segment: Get or set the current segment index for timer-based sequencing.
TimerCHOP.goToNextSegment: Jump to the next predefined segment in the timer sequence.
TimerCHOP.goToPrevSegment: Jump to the previous predefined segment in the timer sequence.
TimerCHOP.goTo: Precisely navigate to a specific time index using various time units like seconds, frames, or fractions.

WaveCHOP.chanIndex: Get the current sample index being evaluated during wave generation.

ActorCOMP_Class.bodies: Returns a list of all the physical bodies currently present in an ActorCOMP simulation.

AnimationCOMP_Class.setKeyframe: Add or modify a keyframe for an animation channel at a specific position, allowing precise control over animation parameters.
AnimationCOMP_Class.deleteKeyframe: Remove a specific keyframe from an animation channel, enabling dynamic animation adjustment.

AnnotateCOMP_Class.height: Retrieve the vertical dimension of an annotation in network editor units.
AnnotateCOMP_Class.width: Get the horizontal dimension of an annotation in network editor units.
AnnotateCOMP_Class.x: Obtain the horizontal position of the annotation's left edge in network editor units.
AnnotateCOMP_Class.y: Get the vertical position of the annotation's bottom edge in network editor units.
AnnotateCOMP_Class.utility: Toggle or check the utility flag that determines special handling of the annotation.

BulletsolverCOMP_Class.actors: Retrieve a list of all Actor COMPs participating in the Bullet physics simulation.

ButtonCOMP_Class.click: Programmatically simulate a mouse click on a button panel, with configurable click parameters.

CameraCOMP_Class.projectionInverse: Calculate the inverse projection matrix for a camera based on specified width and height aspects.
CameraCOMP_Class.projection: Generate the projection matrix for a camera using specified width and height aspects.
CameraCOMP_Class.mpcdiRegion: Generate a dictionary containing camera transform and projection data for MPCDI file creation.

ContainerCOMP_Class.click: Simulate a mouse click at specific coordinates within a container panel.
ContainerCOMP_Class.clickChild: Trigger a click on a sub-panel within a container, useful for interacting with radio buttons.

EngineCOMP_Class.goToDone: Pulse the 'Go to Done' parameter, advancing the engine's state progression.

FieldCOMP_Class.setKeyboardFocus: Set keyboard focus to the Field COMP, optionally selecting all text.

FlexsolverCOMP_Class.actors: Returns a list of all Actor COMPs currently participating in the Flex Solver simulation.

GeometryCOMP.bounds: Calculate the 3D bounding box of the geometry contained in a Geometry COMP. Useful for determining the spatial extent of 3D objects.

GeotextCOMP.textHeight: Get the rendered height of text without padding, specifically in Text mode. Helps determine text dimensions for layout purposes.
GeotextCOMP.textWidth: Get the rendered width of text without padding, specifically in Text mode. Helps determine text dimensions for layout purposes.
GeotextCOMP.evalTextSize: Compute the exact width and height of a given text string based on current font settings. Useful for precise text layout and sizing.

LightCOMP.projectionInverse: Generate the inverse projection matrix for a light's view, essential for advanced rendering and projection calculations.
LightCOMP.projection: Create the projection matrix for a light, used in complex rendering and lighting scenarios.
LightCOMP.mpcdiRegion: Generate a dictionary describing the light's transform and projection for MPCDI (Multi-Projector Calibration) file generation.

ListCOMP.attribs: Access the table-level attributes of a List COMP, enabling programmatic manipulation of list data and appearance.
ListCOMP.focusRow: Retrieve the last row that had keyboard focus for editing, useful for tracking user interaction.
ListCOMP.selectRow: Get the currently selected row in the List COMP, helpful for tracking user selections.
ListCOMP.scroll: Programmatically scroll the List COMP to a specific row and column.
ListCOMP.setKeyboardFocus: Programmatically set keyboard focus to a specific cell in the list, with optional select-all behavior.

NvidiaflexsolverCOMP.actors: Retrieve a list of all Actor COMPs currently participating in the Nvidia Flex physics simulation.

OpviewerCOMP.isViewable: Check if a specific operator can be viewed without causing recursion issues in the operator viewer.

ParameterCOMP.minWidth: Get the minimum width at which the parameter dialog can be drawn before requiring scaling.

SliderCOMP.click: Simulate a mouse click on the slider, allowing programmatic interaction with slider controls.

TimeCOMP.frame: Get or set the current frame output by the Time COMP, enabling precise timeline control.
TimeCOMP.seconds: Get or set the current time in seconds, providing flexible time manipulation.
TimeCOMP.play: Control or check the play state of the timeline, toggling playback on or off.
TimeCOMP.timecode: Get or set the current timecode, useful for synchronization and precise time tracking.

WindowCOMP.scalingMonitorIndex: Retrieve the index of the monitor whose DPI scaling is being used for the window.
WindowCOMP.isOpen: Check whether the window is currently open, useful for tracking window state.
WindowCOMP.contentWidth: Get the width of the window's contents, ignoring borders, in points or pixels.
WindowCOMP.setForeground: Activate the window, bring it to the foreground, and set focus, useful for managing window z-order and interaction.

EvaluateDAT_Class.exprCol: Get the current column index during expression evaluation in a DAT.
EvaluateDAT_Class.exprRow: Get the current row index during expression evaluation in a DAT.
EvaluateDAT_Class.inputCell: Access the current input cell being processed in a DAT expression.
EvaluateDAT_Class.inputCol: Get the current input column index during DAT expression processing.
EvaluateDAT_Class.inputRow: Get the current input row index during DAT expression processing.

FileoutDAT_Class.writeCount: Track the number of times data has been written to a file, useful for generating unique filenames.
FileoutDAT_Class.sendBytes: Write raw byte sequences to a file without adding any terminators.
FileoutDAT_Class.send: Write string messages to a file with an optional terminator.

InsertDAT_Class.subRow: Get the current row index during DAT insert operations.
InsertDAT_Class.subCol: Get the current column index during DAT insert operations.
InsertDAT_Class.fillName: Retrieve the current header name during DAT insert operations.

MediafileinfoDAT_Class.acodec: Get the audio codec of a media file.
MediafileinfoDAT_Class.filename: Retrieve the name of the loaded media file.
MediafileinfoDAT_Class.fps: Get the frame rate of the media file.
MediafileinfoDAT_Class.height: Get the pixel height of the media file.
MediafileinfoDAT_Class.width: Get the pixel width of the media file.
MediafileinfoDAT_Class.fetchInfo: Extract comprehensive metadata information about a media file.

MqttclientDAT_Class.isConnected: Check if the MQTT client is currently connected to a broker.
MqttclientDAT_Class.publish: Send a message to a specific MQTT topic.
MqttclientDAT_Class.subscribe: Subscribe to receive messages from a specific MQTT topic.
MqttclientDAT_Class.unsubscribe: Stop receiving messages from a previously subscribed MQTT topic.

OpfindDAT_Class.onInitGetColumnNames: Custom callback to define column names during operator finding.
OpfindDAT_Class.onFindOPGetValues: Custom callback to retrieve values for found operators.
OpfindDAT_Class.onFindOPGetInclude: Custom callback to determine if an operator should be included in results.

OscinDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte data through an OSC input connection.
OscinDAT_Class.sendOSC: Send formatted OSC messages with flexible parameter options.
OscinDAT_Class.send: Send string messages through an OSC input connection.

OscoutDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte data through an OSC output connection.
OscoutDAT_Class.sendOSC: Send formatted OSC messages with flexible parameter options.
OscoutDAT_Class.send: Send string messages through an OSC output connection.

RenderpickDAT_Class.pick: Perform a rendering pick at specified normalized coordinates, optionally specifying a camera.

ScriptDAT_Class.textFormat: Toggle between table and text formats in a script DAT.
ScriptDAT_Class.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page to an operator.
ScriptDAT_Class.destroyCustomPars: Remove all custom parameters from an operator.
ScriptDAT_Class.sortCustomPages: Reorder custom parameter pages for an operator.

SerialDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte sequences over a serial connection without appending terminators. Useful for low-level communication protocols.
SerialDAT_Class.send: Send string messages through a serial connection with optional terminator characters. Ideal for text-based serial communications.

SocketioDAT_Class.emit: Broadcast an event with optional data to a connected socket.io server. Used for real-time, event-driven communication.

TableDAT_Class.subRow: Retrieves the current row index during table expression evaluation. Helpful for dynamic table processing and references.
TableDAT_Class.subCol: Gets the current column index during table expression evaluation. Useful for context-aware table manipulations.
TableDAT_Class.fillName: Returns the header name for the current table expression context. Enables dynamic header-based operations.

TcpipDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte sequences over a TCP connection without appending terminators. Useful for low-level network communication.
TcpipDAT_Class.send: Send string messages through a TCP connection with optional terminator characters. Ideal for text-based network protocols.

TextDAT_Class.textFormat: Toggle between table and text formats for a TextDAT. Enables flexible text and tabular data transformations.

UdpinDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte sequences through a UDP connection. Useful for lightweight, connectionless network communication.
UdpinDAT_Class.sendOSC: Send Open Sound Control (OSC) formatted messages with flexible type and precision options. Great for multimedia and audio networking.
UdpinDAT_Class.send: Send string messages through a UDP connection. Ideal for simple, non-formatted network communications.

UdtinDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte sequences through a UDP connection. Useful for lightweight, connectionless network communication.
UdtinDAT_Class.sendOSC: Send Open Sound Control (OSC) formatted messages with flexible type and precision options. Great for multimedia and audio networking.
UdtinDAT_Class.send: Send string messages through a UDP connection. Ideal for simple, non-formatted network communications.

WebclientDAT_Class.connections: Get a list of active HTTP connection identifiers. Useful for tracking and managing multiple concurrent web requests.
WebclientDAT_Class.request: Send complex HTTP requests with extensive configuration options. Supports authentication, headers, data, and file uploads.
WebclientDAT_Class.closeConnection: Explicitly close an active web connection. Helps manage network resource cleanup.

WebrtcDAT_Class.openConnection: Initiate a new WebRTC peer connection and receive a unique connection identifier. Essential for establishing real-time communication channels.
WebrtcDAT_Class.sendBytes: Send binary data through a WebRTC data channel. Ideal for low-level, real-time peer-to-peer communication.
WebrtcDAT_Class.sendText: Send text data through a WebRTC data channel. Used for messaging and signaling in peer-to-peer applications.
WebrtcDAT_Class.getStats: Retrieve detailed connection statistics as JSON. Helps monitor WebRTC connection performance and diagnostics.

WebserverDAT_Class.authenticateBasic: Perform Basic Authentication by validating credentials against a provided dictionary. Enables secure web endpoint access.
WebserverDAT_Class.webSocketSendText: Send text data to a connected WebSocket client. Used for real-time, bidirectional web communication.
WebserverDAT_Class.webSocketSendBinary: Send binary data to a connected WebSocket client. Supports complex data transmission over WebSockets.

WebsocketDAT.sendText: Send a text message over an active WebSocket connection. Useful for real-time communication and data transmission in web-based interactive projects.
WebsocketDAT.sendPong: Send a pong response in a WebSocket connection to maintain the connection and acknowledge a ping request. Helps manage WebSocket connection health and responsiveness.
WebsocketDAT.sendPing: Send a ping request over a WebSocket connection to check the connection status and latency. Useful for monitoring WebSocket connection stability.
WebsocketDAT.sendBinary: Transmit binary data over a WebSocket connection. Ideal for sending non-text data like images, audio, or custom binary protocols in interactive applications.

GlslMAT.compileResult: Retrieve the most recent shader compilation result as a string. Useful for debugging shader compilation errors or checking shader status.

PbrMAT.outputShader: Generate a new GLSL shader from the current PBR material settings. Optionally include geometry shader and instance texturing for advanced rendering scenarios.

PhongMAT.outputShader: Create a new GLSL shader based on the current Phong material configuration. Allows optional geometry shader generation and instance texturing support.

AbsTime_Class.frame: Get the total number of frames played since TouchDesigner started, unaffected by pausing.
AbsTime_Class.seconds: Retrieve the total seconds elapsed since TouchDesigner's launch, unaffected by pausing.
AbsTime_Class.step: Get the number of frames elapsed between previous and current frames, with values > 1 indicating frame dropping.
AbsTime_Class.stepSeconds: Get the time elapsed between previous and current frames in absolute seconds.

App_Class.applicationsFolder: Get the primary system folder for installing applications, typically 'C:/Program Files'.
App_Class.architecture: Retrieve the application's compile architecture, usually 32 or 64 bit.
App_Class.binFolder: Get the installation folder containing TouchDesigner's binary executables.
App_Class.build: Get the specific build number of the TouchDesigner application.
App_Class.compileDate: Retrieve the date the application was compiled as a tuple of (year, month, day).
App_Class.configFolder: Get the installation folder containing configuration files.
App_Class.desktopFolder: Get the current user's desktop folder path.
App_Class.enableCachedParameters: Toggle caching of parameter values instead of constantly re-evaluating.
App_Class.enableOptimizedExprs: Enable or disable Python expression optimization, which defaults to True on startup.
App_Class.experimental: Check if the current TouchDesigner instance is an experimental build.
App_Class.installFolder: Get the main TouchDesigner installation directory.
App_Class.osName: Get the name of the operating system running TouchDesigner.
App_Class.osVersion: Get the version of the operating system.
App_Class.power: Control the overall processing state, enabling or halting all processing when set to True or False.
App_Class.product: Determine the type of executable running, such as 'TouchDesigner', 'TouchPlayer', or 'TouchEngine'.
App_Class.systemFolder: Get the installation folder containing system files.
App_Class.tempFolder: Get the system's temporary folder path.
App_Class.addNonCommercialLimit: Restrict the application to operate at a non-commercial license level.
App_Class.removeNonCommercialLimit: Remove previously added non-commercial license restrictions.
App_Class.addResolutionLimit: Limit all textures to a specified maximum resolution.
App_Class.removeResolutionLimit: Remove previously set texture resolution limits.

Attribute_Class.name: Get the name of the attribute.
Attribute_Class.size: Get the number of values associated with this attribute.
Attribute_Class.isArray: Check if the attribute is an array type.
Attribute_Class.arraySize: Get the size of the attribute array, returning 0 for non-array attributes.
Attribute_Class.numMatCols: Get the number of columns for matrix attributes.
Attribute_Class.numMatRows: Get the number of rows for matrix attributes.
Attribute_Class.destroy: Permanently remove the referenced attribute from the system.
Attribute_Class.vals: Retrieve all attribute values as a list, with optional delayed evaluation.

Bezier_Class.anchors: Get the list of anchor points defining the Bezier curve.
Bezier_Class.basis: Retrieve the Bezier curve's basis as a list of float values.
Bezier_Class.closed: Determine or set whether the curve is closed or open.
Bezier_Class.order: Get the mathematical order of the Bezier curve (one more than its degree).
Bezier_Class.segments: Get a list of curve segments, where each segment is a list of control points.
Bezier_Class.tangents: Retrieve the tangent vectors for the Bezier curve.
Bezier_Class.updateAnchor: Modify a specific anchor point's position, with optional tangent adjustments.
Bezier_Class.updateTangent: Modify a tangent vertex with optional rotation and scaling controls.
Bezier_Class.deleteAnchor: Remove a specific anchor point and its neighboring tangents from the curve.

Body_Class.index: Get the index of this body within its physics simulation.
Body_Class.rotate: Get or set the body's rotation in world space coordinates.
Body_Class.translate: Get or set the body's position in world space coordinates.
Body_Class.angularVelocity: Get or set the rotational velocity of the physics body.
Body_Class.linearVelocity: Get or set the translational velocity of the physics body.
Body_Class.applyImpulseForce: Apply an instantaneous force to a body in a Bullet physics simulation.
Body_Class.applyTorque: Apply rotational force to a body for a single physics simulation frame.
Body_Class.applyImpulseTorque: Apply an instantaneous rotational force to a physics body.
Body_Class.applyForce: Apply a directional force to a body for a single physics simulation frame.

Group_Class.default: Returns a tuple of default group points, providing access to the initial configuration of a group.
Group_Class.name: Get or set the unique identifier name for a point or primitive group.
Group_Class.destroy: Permanently removes the current point or primitive group from the operator.

ListAttribute_Class.text: Get or set the text contents of a list attribute cell.
ListAttribute_Class.textColor: Configure the text color as an RGBA tuple for visual customization.
ListAttribute_Class.textJustify: Control text alignment within the cell using predefined justification types.
ListAttribute_Class.help: Set or retrieve a help tooltip that appears when hovering over the cell.
ListAttribute_Class.fontFace: Specify the font typeface for rendering text in the cell.
ListAttribute_Class.editable: Determine whether the cell's contents can be directly edited by the user.

Matrix_Class.vals: Access or modify the underlying numerical values of the matrix.
Matrix_Class.rows: Retrieve a list representation of the matrix's rows.
Matrix_Class.transpose: Swap the matrix's rows and columns in-place, modifying the original matrix.
Matrix_Class.invert: Calculate and apply the matrix's multiplicative inverse to itself.
Matrix_Class.copy: Create a new matrix with identical values to the current matrix.
Matrix_Class.rotate: Apply rotation transformations to the matrix around X, Y, or Z axes.

Mesh_Class.closedU: Indicates whether the mesh is closed along its U parameter, forming a continuous surface.
Mesh_Class.numRows: Get the total number of rows in the mesh geometry.
Mesh_Class.center: Retrieve or set the barycentric center point of the mesh primitive.
Mesh_Class.normal: Calculate the normalized vector perpendicular to the mesh surface.
Mesh_Class.destroy: Remove the current mesh primitive, optionally deleting associated points.

Monitor_Class.index: Get the sequential position of this monitor in the system's display configuration.
Monitor_Class.isPrimary: Determine if this monitor is set as the primary display device.
Monitor_Class.width: Retrieve the horizontal pixel resolution of the monitor.
Monitor_Class.dpiScale: Get the display scaling factor for high-resolution screens.
Monitor_Class.refreshRate: Obtain the current vertical refresh rate of the monitor.

NetworkEditor_Class.showNetworkOverview: Toggle the visibility of the network topology overview panel.
NetworkEditor_Class.zoom: Control the magnification level of the network editor view.
NetworkEditor_Class.x: Set or get the horizontal scroll position of the network editor.
NetworkEditor_Class.fitWidth: Automatically adjust the view to accommodate a specified network width.
NetworkEditor_Class.home: Reset the network view to show all operators or selected operators.

Options_Class.[]: Directly access or modify specific configuration options by name.
Options_Class.resetToDefaults: Restore all options to their original factory settings.

Page_Class.name: Get or set the descriptive name of a parameter page.
Page_Class.pars: Retrieve a list of all parameters contained within the page.
Page_Class.destroy: Permanently remove the entire parameter page and its contents.
Page_Class.sort: Reorder custom parameters or parameter groups manually.

Pane_Class.id: Get a unique numeric identifier for the pane.
Pane_Class.name: Set or retrieve the descriptive name of the pane.
Pane_Class.close: Shut down the current pane window.
Pane_Class.tearAway: Detach the pane into a standalone floating window.

PanelValue_Class.name: Retrieve the unique identifier of the panel value.
PanelValue_Class.val: Get or set the current value stored in the panel value object.
PanelValue_Class.valid: Check whether the panel value still exists or has been deleted.

Panes_Class.len: Return the total number of panes in the TouchDesigner user interface.

Par_Class.val: Get or set the current value of a parameter directly, bypassing any expressions or evaluation modes.
Par_Class.expr: Get or set the raw expression of a parameter without evaluating its current value.
Par_Class.enable: Toggle whether a parameter is enabled or disabled, controlling its interactivity and evaluation.
Par_Class.name: Get or set the unique identifier name for a parameter within an operator.
Par_Class.min: Set the minimum numerical value for a parameter, useful for constraining numerical inputs.
Par_Class.max: Set the maximum numerical value for a parameter, useful for constraining numerical inputs.
Par_Class.mode: Get or set the evaluation mode for a parameter, determining how its value is computed.
Par_Class.default: Get or set the default value for a parameter, which can be restored using reset().
Par_Class.eval: Evaluate a parameter and return its current value, taking into account expressions and evaluation modes.
Par_Class.pulse: Temporarily set a parameter to a specific value for a single frame or time period.
Par_Class.reset: Restore a parameter to its default state, clearing any custom expressions or values.

ParGroup_Class.bindExpr: Set expressions that bind parameter values to external objects or references.
ParGroup_Class.default: Get or set default values for a group of parameters simultaneously.
ParGroup_Class.enable: Enable or disable an entire group of parameters with a single action.
ParGroup_Class.val: Get or set values for a group of parameters in a single operation.
ParGroup_Class.eval: Evaluate an entire parameter group and return its current values.
ParGroup_Class.reset: Reset all parameters in a group to their default states.

ParGroupPulse_Class.pulse: Pulse values for an entire parameter group, useful for triggering synchronized actions.

ParGroupUnit_Class.unit: Access the unit parameter within a ParGroupUnit object.

Peer_Class.port: Get the network port associated with a peer connection.
Peer_Class.address: Retrieve the network address of a connected peer.
Peer_Class.sendBytes: Send raw byte messages through a peer connection.
Peer_Class.sendOSC: Send Open Sound Control (OSC) formatted messages through a network connection.

Point_Class.x: Get or set the X coordinate of a point in 3D space.
Point_Class.y: Get or set the Y coordinate of a point in 3D space.
Point_Class.z: Get or set the Z coordinate of a point in 3D space.
Point_Class.destroy: Remove a specific point from a geometric primitive.

Points_Class.len: Return the total number of points in a geometric collection.

Poly_Class.closed: Check whether a polygon is closed or open.
Poly_Class.center: Get or set the barycentric center coordinate of a primitive.
Poly_Class.normal: Retrieve the calculated normal vector for a primitive.
Poly_Class.destroy: Remove a specific primitive from a geometric collection.

Position_Class.x: Get or set the X component of a 3D position.
Position_Class.y: Get or set the Y component of a 3D position.
Position_Class.z: Get or set the Z component of a 3D position.
Position_Class.translate: Move a position by specified X, Y, Z offsets.
Position_Class.scale: Scale a position's components by specified factors.

Prim_Class.center: Get or set the barycentric coordinate of a primitive as a TDU.Position object, representing its central location.
Prim_Class.index: Retrieve the zero-based index of a primitive within its list of primitives.
Prim_Class.normal: Get the calculated normal vector for this primitive, representing its geometric orientation.
Prim_Class.weight: Retrieve the associated weight value for this primitive, used in certain geometric calculations.
Prim_Class.direction: Get a normalized vector pointing from the centroid of the primitive.
Prim_Class.min: Obtain the minimum coordinate values for this primitive along each dimension.
Prim_Class.max: Obtain the maximum coordinate values for this primitive along each dimension.
Prim_Class.size: Calculate and return the dimensional size of the primitive as a TDU.Position object.
Prim_Class.destroy: Remove and destroy the current primitive, optionally removing associated points.
Prim_Class.eval: Evaluate the primitive's position at specific parametric coordinates (u, v).
Prim_Class.len: Return the total number of vertices in this primitive.

Prims_Class.[]: Access a specific primitive by integer index within the collection.
Prims_Class.Iterator: Iterate through each primitive in the collection.
Prims_Class.len: Return the total number of primitives in the collection.

ProgressiveUnload_Class.active: Indicates whether a progressive unloading process is currently in progress.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.remainingNodes: Get the number of nodes left to be unloaded during the progressive unloading process.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.nodesUnloaded: Retrieve the total number of nodes that have been unloaded.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.lastFrameUnloadTime: Get the unload time in milliseconds for the most recent frame.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.averageFrameUnloadTime: Calculate the average unload time across all previous frames.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.gpuMemUnloaded: Return the total GPU memory in megabytes that has been unloaded.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.elapsedFrames: Get the number of frames that have passed since progressive unloading started.
ProgressiveUnload_Class.kill: Immediately terminate the ongoing progressive unloading operation.

ProgressiveUnloader_Class.active: Indicates whether a progressive unloading process is currently in progress.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.remainingNodes: Get the number of nodes left to be unloaded during the progressive unloading process.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.nodesUnloaded: Retrieve the total number of nodes that have been unloaded.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.lastFrameUnloadTime: Get the unload time in milliseconds for the most recent frame.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.averageFrameUnloadTime: Calculate the average unload time across all previous frames.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.gpuMemUnloaded: Return the total GPU memory in megabytes that has been unloaded.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.elapsedFrames: Get the number of frames that have passed since progressive unloading started.
ProgressiveUnloader_Class.kill: Immediately terminate the ongoing progressive unloading operation.

Project_Class.folder: Get the directory path where the current project is located.
Project_Class.name: Retrieve the filename of the current TouchDesigner project.
Project_Class.saveVersion: Get the version information of the saved project.
Project_Class.saveTime: Get the timestamp of when the project was last saved.
Project_Class.cookRate: Control the maximum number of frames processed per second.
Project_Class.realTime: Toggle whether frames can be skipped to maintain cook rate.
Project_Class.save: Save the current TouchDesigner project to disk, with optional external TOX saving.
Project_Class.load: Load a specific TouchDesigner project file.
Project_Class.quit: Close the current TouchDesigner project, with optional force or crash modes.

Quaternion_Class.x: Get or set the x component of the quaternion rotation.
Quaternion_Class.y: Get or set the y component of the quaternion rotation.
Quaternion_Class.z: Get or set the z component of the quaternion rotation.
Quaternion_Class.w: Get or set the w component of the quaternion rotation.
Quaternion_Class.lerp: Perform linear interpolation between two quaternions.
Quaternion_Class.slerp: Perform spherical linear interpolation between two quaternions.
Quaternion_Class.rotate: Apply the quaternion's rotation to a vector.
Quaternion_Class.eulerAngles: Convert quaternion rotation to euler angles in degrees.
Quaternion_Class.fromEuler: Create a quaternion from euler angle rotations.

Run_Class.active: Control whether the scheduled script will execute.
Run_Class.group: Get or set a group label for the scheduled script.
Run_Class.remainingFrames: Track or set the number of frames before script execution.
Run_Class.remainingMilliseconds: Track or set the milliseconds before script execution.
Run_Class.kill: Cancel and remove a scheduled script before it executes.

Segment_Class.beginFrames: Get the starting point of a segment in frame units.
Segment_Class.beginSeconds: Get the starting point of a segment in seconds.
Segment_Class.lengthFrames: Retrieve the total length of the segment in frames.
Segment_Class.lengthSeconds: Retrieve the total length of the segment in seconds.
Segment_Class.cycle: Determine whether the segment will repeat itself.
Segment_Class.speed: Get the playback speed multiplier for the segment.

Sequence_Class.blockSize: Get or set the number of parameters in each sequence block.
Sequence_Class.numBlocks: Control the total number of parameter blocks in the sequence.
Sequence_Class.insertBlock: Add a new parameter block to the sequence at a specific location.
Sequence_Class.moveBlock: Relocate parameter blocks within the sequence.
Sequence_Class.sortBlocks: Reorder sequence blocks based on a custom sorting criteria.

SequenceBlock_Class.index: Get the numeric index of the current parameter block.
SequenceBlock_Class.name: Get or set the name of the sequence block.
SequenceBlock_Class.destroy: Remove the current parameter block from its sequence.

SysInfo.numCPUs: Get the total number of CPU cores available on the system, useful for understanding hardware capabilities and potential parallel processing.
SysInfo.ram: Retrieve the amount of available RAM memory, helpful for understanding system resources and potential performance constraints.
SysInfo.numMonitors: Get the number of connected display monitors, useful for multi-screen setup detection and display configuration.
SysInfo.xres: Retrieve the current horizontal screen resolution of the system's primary monitor.
SysInfo.yres: Retrieve the current vertical screen resolution of the system's primary monitor.
SysInfo.tfs: Get the path to the TouchDesigner file system directory, useful for locating default resources and configuration files.
SysInfo.MIDIInputs: Get a list of all available MIDI input device names connected to the system.
SysInfo.MIDIOutputs: Get a list of all available MIDI output device names connected to the system.
SysInfo.GPUName: Retrieve the name of the currently selected graphics processing unit.
SysInfo.GPUID: Get the unique identifier (UUID) of the selected graphics card.
SysInfo.GPUPlatformID: Retrieve the operating system identifier for the selected GPU.

TDU.fileTypes: Access a comprehensive dictionary of all supported file types organized by category.
TDU.rand: Generate a reproducible random float between 0 and 1 based on a seed value, useful for consistent randomization.
TDU.clamp: Restrict a numeric value between a minimum and maximum, preventing values from exceeding specified bounds.
TDU.remap: Transform a value from one numerical range to another, useful for scaling and mapping values.
TDU.base: Extract the non-numeric prefix from a string, helpful for parsing text with numeric suffixes.
TDU.digits: Extract the numeric portion from a string, useful for parsing filenames or text with embedded numbers.
TDU.validName: Convert a string into a valid TouchDesigner operator name by replacing illegal characters.
TDU.validPath: Transform a string into a valid file path, sanitizing and replacing problematic characters.
TDU.expand: Expand a pattern into a list of matching items, supporting wildcard and pattern matching.
TDU.expandPath: Resolve a file path using project paths and current folder context.
TDU.collapsePath: Compress a file path using project paths, creating a more compact representation.
TDU.split: Convert a space-separated string into a list, with support for quote-delimited elements.
TDU.match: Filter a list of items based on a wildcard pattern, with optional case sensitivity.
TDU.tryExcept: Execute a function and return a fallback value or result if an exception occurs.

Timecode.countdown: Calculate the remaining time from a specified length, useful for timer and duration tracking.
Timecode.fps: Get or set the frames per second for precise time and animation calculations.
Timecode.text: Retrieve the formatted timecode string representation, typically in 'hh:mm:ss:ff' format.
Timecode.setComponents: Manually set timecode values using individual time components like hours, minutes, seconds.
Timecode.setString: Initialize timecode from a formatted string, supporting negative and custom frame rates.
Timecode.setTotalFrames: Set timecode duration using a total number of frames, enabling precise time manipulation.

UI.clipboard: Get or set the operating system clipboard text, enabling cross-application text transfer.
UI.messageBox: Display a configurable dialog box with custom title, message, and button options.
UI.chooseFile: Open a file selection dialog for loading or saving files with optional type filtering.
UI.viewFile: Open a file or URL in the default system application.
UI.refresh: Immediately update and redraw all TouchDesigner UI elements and viewports.
UI.openCOMPEditor: Open the component editor for a specific operator, enabling direct editing.

Vertex.index: Get the position of the vertex within its primitive, useful for geometric manipulation.
Vertex.prim: Access the parent primitive of the vertex, enabling detailed geometry interactions.

VFS.addFile: Add an embedded file to a component from disk, with optional custom naming.
VFS.export: Export virtual files to a specified folder, supporting pattern matching.
VFS.find: Search for files within the virtual file system using wildcard patterns.

VFSFile.name: Get or set the name of a virtual file, supporting hierarchical file naming.
VFSFile.size: Retrieve the total size of the file data in bytes.
VFSFile.byteArray: Access or modify the file's raw byte data directly.
VFSFile.export: Save the virtual file to a specified folder on the physical disk.

FileoutPOP_Class.fileSuffix: Returns the generated file suffix for output files, which helps uniquely identify exported media based on current rendering settings and parameters.
FileoutPOP_Class.save: Immediately save the current frame or image data from the File Out TOP to disk using the configured output settings and file path.

CopySOP_Class.copyIndex: Get the current index of the copy being processed, starting at zero during geometry copying operations.
CopySOP_Class.copyTotal: Retrieve the total number of copies being created by the CopySOP during geometry replication.
CopySOP_Class.inputPoint: Access the current input point being processed during geometry copy transformations.

DeleteSOP_Class.inputPoint: Get the current input point being processed during point deletion operations.
DeleteSOP_Class.inputPrim: Access the current input primitive being processed during deletion procedures.

GroupSOP_Class.inputPrim: Get the current input primitive being processed during group manipulation operations.

ModelSOP_Class.appendPoly: Create and add a new polygon with a specified number of vertices to the geometry, with options for closure and point generation.
ModelSOP_Class.appendBezier: Add a new Bezier curve to the geometry with configurable vertex count, closure, and order.
ModelSOP_Class.appendMesh: Generate a new mesh surface with specified rows and columns, with options for U and V closure.
ModelSOP_Class.clear: Remove all existing geometry from the SOP, resetting it to an empty state.
ModelSOP_Class.copy: Copy geometry from another SOP, completely replacing the current geometry.

PointSOP_Class.inputColor: Retrieve the current point color being evaluated from the first input, returned as a 4-tuple RGBA value.
PointSOP_Class.inputColor2: Get the point color from the second input as a 4-tuple RGBA value during point processing.
PointSOP_Class.inputNormal: Access the current point normal from the first input as a 3-tuple vector.
PointSOP_Class.inputNormal2: Retrieve the point normal from the second input as a 3-tuple vector.
PointSOP_Class.inputTexture: Get the current point texture coordinates from the first input as a 3-tuple (u, v, w).
PointSOP_Class.inputTexture2: Access texture coordinates from the second input as a 3-tuple (u, v, w).

PrimitiveSOP_Class.inputColor: Retrieve the current primitive color as a 4-tuple RGBA value during primitive processing.
PrimitiveSOP_Class.inputPrim: Access the current input primitive being processed.

ScriptSOP_Class.clear: Remove all existing geometry from the ScriptSOP, resetting it to an empty state.
ScriptSOP_Class.copy: Copy geometry from another SOP, completely replacing the current geometry.
ScriptSOP_Class.appendPoly: Create and add a new polygon with specified vertices to the geometry.
ScriptSOP_Class.appendBezier: Add a new Bezier curve to the geometry with configurable parameters.
ScriptSOP_Class.appendMesh: Generate a new mesh surface with specified dimensions and closure options.
ScriptSOP_Class.createPointGroup: Create a new point group with the specified name in the SOP.
ScriptSOP_Class.createPrimGroup: Create a new primitive group with the specified name.
ScriptSOP_Class.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page to the operator.
ScriptSOP_Class.destroyCustomPars: Remove all custom parameters from the operator.
ScriptSOP_Class.sortCustomPages: Reorder custom parameter pages based on the provided page order.

TextSOP_Class.numLines: Get the total number of lines in the text after word-wrapping and text processing.
TextSOP_Class.ascender: Retrieve the font's ascender metric, representing the height above the baseline.
TextSOP_Class.descender: Get the font's descender metric, representing the depth below the baseline.
TextSOP_Class.capHeight: Access the height of capital letters, typically based on a capital H.
TextSOP_Class.xHeight: Retrieve the height of lowercase letters, usually measured from the lowercase x.
TextSOP_Class.lineGap: Get the recommended vertical spacing between text lines according to font metrics.
TextSOP_Class.numGlyphs: Obtain the number of glyphs generated during text processing.
TextSOP_Class.fontSupportsChars: Check if the current font supports all characters in the given string.
TextSOP_Class.lines: Retrieve a list of TextLine objects representing the processed text lines.

GlslmultiTOP_Class.compileResult: Retrieve the most recent compilation result for a multi-pass GLSL shader, useful for debugging shader compilation errors.

GlslTOP_Class.compileResult: Get the latest compilation result for a GLSL shader, helping developers identify and troubleshoot shader compilation issues.

KinectazureTOP_Class.colorCameraIntrinsics: Retrieve the intrinsic camera parameters for the color camera, providing detailed information about camera lens and sensor characteristics.
KinectazureTOP_Class.depthCameraIntrinsics: Access the intrinsic parameters of the depth camera, essential for precise depth mapping and spatial understanding.
KinectazureTOP_Class.getRemapTransform: Generate a transformation matrix between different coordinate spaces of Kinect Azure devices, useful for advanced spatial mapping and alignment.
KinectazureTOP_Class.transformWorldToColorImage: Convert a 3D world position to 2D coordinates in the color camera image, enabling precise pixel mapping from world space.
KinectazureTOP_Class.transformWorldToDepthImage: Transform a 3D world position to 2D coordinates in the depth camera image, facilitating depth-based spatial analysis.

MoviefileinTOP_Class.fileStartTimecode: Get the starting timecode of a video file, helpful for precise video synchronization and metadata tracking.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.metadata: Access a comprehensive dictionary of video file metadata, providing detailed information about the media file.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.fileHeight: Retrieve the pixel height of the loaded movie file, useful for image processing and sizing operations.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.fileWidth: Get the pixel width of the loaded movie file, essential for understanding image dimensions and scaling.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.hasAudio: Check if the movie file contains audio tracks, useful for multimedia file validation.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.index: Get the current playback position in the movie file, enabling precise frame navigation.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.numImages: Determine the total number of frames in the movie file, helpful for playback and processing control.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.findHeader: Search and retrieve a specific header value from the movie file's metadata, useful for extracting custom file information.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.unload: Unload the movie file and free memory, helping manage system resources during complex media workflows.
MoviefileinTOP_Class.preload: Prepare the movie file by opening and pre-reading initial frames, optimizing playback performance.

MoviefileoutTOP_Class.fileSuffix: Generate a unique file suffix for output movies, allowing for automatic file naming and organization.

MpcdiTOP_Class.cameraTransform: Retrieve the camera transformation matrix for multi-projector configurations, essential for advanced projection mapping.
MpcdiTOP_Class.projection: Get the projection matrix for precise alignment in complex display setups.

NotchTOP_Class.exposedProperties: Access a dictionary of properties imported from a Notch block, facilitating integration of Notch visual effects.

OakselectTOP_Class.timecode: Retrieve the master timecode from an OAK device, enabling precise synchronization and timing control.

PhotoshopinTOP_Class.isConnected: Check if the operator is successfully connected to a running Photoshop instance, useful for verifying external software integration.
PhotoshopinTOP_Class.isReceivingUpdates: Determine if the operator is actively receiving image updates from Photoshop, helping manage real-time image synchronization.

ScalabledisplayTOP_Class.cameraTransform: Get the camera transformation matrix for scalable display configurations, crucial for advanced projection setups.
ScalabledisplayTOP_Class.projection: Retrieve the projection matrix for precise multi-display alignment.
ScalabledisplayTOP_Class.calibWidth: Access the calibration width defined in the calibration file, useful for display configuration and sizing.
ScalabledisplayTOP_Class.calibHeight: Get the calibration height from the calibration file, essential for precise display setup.

ScriptTOP_Class.copyNumpyArray: Copy a NumPy array's contents directly into a TOP's texture, useful for transferring image data programmatically.
ScriptTOP_Class.copyCUDAMemory: Transfer CUDA memory contents to the TOP, allowing direct GPU-to-GPU image data transfer.
ScriptTOP_Class.loadByteArray: Load a compressed image file from a byte array directly into the TOP, enabling dynamic image loading.
ScriptTOP_Class.appendCustomPage: Add a new custom parameter page to the operator, allowing dynamic extension of its configuration.
ScriptTOP_Class.destroyCustomPars: Remove all custom parameters from the operator, resetting its parameter configuration.
ScriptTOP_Class.sortCustomPages: Reorder custom parameter pages, providing control over the parameter UI layout.

St2110inTOP_Class.inputSignalFormat: Retrieve the current input signal format for ST2110 video streams, useful for checking video configuration.
St2110inTOP_Class.isConnected: Check if a video device is actively streaming to the operator, verifying connection status.

TextTOP_Class.curText: Get the current text contents of the TextTOP, useful for reading dynamic text.
TextTOP_Class.cursorEnd: Get or set the cursor end position for text selection or manipulation.
TextTOP_Class.cursorStart: Get or set the cursor start position for text selection or manipulation.
TextTOP_Class.selectedText: Retrieve the currently selected text content.
TextTOP_Class.textHeight: Get the calculated pixel height of rendered text, accounting for font and formatting.
TextTOP_Class.textWidth: Get the calculated pixel width of rendered text, accounting for font and formatting.
TextTOP_Class.numLines: Determine the number of text lines after word-wrapping and formatting.
TextTOP_Class.ascender: Get the font's ascender value, representing the height above the baseline.
TextTOP_Class.descender: Get the font's descender value, representing the depth below the baseline.
TextTOP_Class.capHeight: Get the height of capital letters in the font, typically based on a capital H.
TextTOP_Class.xHeight: Get the height of lowercase letters in the font, typically based on a lowercase x.
TextTOP_Class.lineGap: Retrieve the recommended spacing between text lines according to font metrics.
TextTOP_Class.fontSupportsChars: Check if a font contains glyphs for all characters in a given string.
TextTOP_Class.evalTextSize: Calculate the width and height of a text string using the current text settings.
TextTOP_Class.lines: Get a list of text lines with detailed rendering information.

VideodeviceinTOP_Class.isConnected: Check if a video input device is currently streaming to the operator.
VideodeviceinTOP_Class.inputSignalFormat: Retrieve the input video signal format for the connected device.
VideodeviceinTOP_Class.timecode: Access the timecode information for the latest received video frame.

VideostreaminTOP_Class.connectionsFailed: Track the number of failed connection attempts to the video stream.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.connectionsLost: Monitor the number of previously established connections that were lost.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.frameTime: Get the timestamp of the currently displayed video frame.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.isConnected: Check if the operator is currently connected to the video stream URL.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.isConnecting: Determine if the operator is attempting to establish a connection.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.isOddField: Check which field is currently being displayed during de-interlacing.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.videoHeight: Get the pixel height of the video stream.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.videoWidth: Get the pixel width of the video stream.
VideostreaminTOP_Class.unload: Unload the current video stream and free associated memory.

VideostreamoutTOP_Class.streamURL: Retrieve the URL used for streaming the video output.

WebrenderTOP_Class.loaded: Check if the current webpage has finished loading.
WebrenderTOP_Class.interactMouse: Simulate mouse interactions like clicks, moves, and drags on the webpage.
WebrenderTOP_Class.interactTouch: Simulate touch events on the webpage.
WebrenderTOP_Class.executeJavaScript: Run JavaScript code directly on the current webpage.
WebrenderTOP_Class.sendKey: Send keyboard input events to the webpage.
WebrenderTOP_Class.sendString: Send a string of characters to the webpage.
WebrenderTOP_Class.flushCookieStore: Clear the current browser cookies and local storage.

ZedTOP_Class.calibrationParameters: Get the processed stereo camera calibration parameters for rectified images.
ZedTOP_Class.calibrationParametersRaw: Get the raw stereo camera calibration parameters before image rectification.
